informal_grounds.summary: T040 CLI thinning — error type for BaselineCaptureService::run; aggregates port-level failures (symlink rejection, invalid track-id, layer-bindings load failure, capture failure) surfaced to the CLI without leaking internal infrastructure details

BaselineCaptureInteractor.docs: Interactor implementing BaselineCaptureService. Orchestrates symlink guards, track-id validation, layer-bindings resolution, and per-layer rustdoc baseline capture. All I/O is performed via injected ports (no direct infrastructure calls).

informal_grounds.summary: T040 CLI thinning — usecase application-service interactor for `sotp track baseline-capture`; moves baseline-capture orchestration out of the CLI handler into the usecase layer so the CLI becomes a thin adapter over port-based dependency injection

T040 CLI thinning — CQRS Command carrying all input parameters for BaselineCaptureService::run; enables the CLI handler to validate and forward CLI flags as a typed command without embedding orchestration logic

CatalogueImplSignalsReport.docs: Result of a completed catalogue-impl-signals evaluation run. Carries the formatted markdown text and a pre-computed any_red flag so callers do not need to re-parse the report string.

informal_grounds.summary: T040 CLI thinning addendum — structured return value (markdown text + any_red boolean) replacing previous String-only return from CatalogueImplSignalsService::run; eliminates CLI-side markdown string re-parsing to detect failure

PreCommitTypeSignalsInteractor.docs: Existing interactor implementing PreCommitTypeSignalsService. Referenced for continuity.

ReviewCheckApprovedInteractor.docs: Concrete struct implementing ReviewCheckApprovedService. Modified in T040 thinning: build_fn field (CheckApprovedBuildFn type alias) replaces earlier direct domain imports at the CLI boundary.

informal_grounds.summary: T040 CLI thinning — ReviewCheckApprovedInteractor is a pre-existing interactor modified to accept a build_fn closure replacing earlier direct domain imports at the CLI boundary; modification removes the CLI-to-domain direct dependency while preserving the review check-approved use case

TaskOperationInteractor.docs: Concrete generic struct implementing TaskOperationService. Modified in T040 thinning: generic over S: TrackReader + TrackWriter + ImplPlanReader + ImplPlanWriter + Send + Sync with injected branch_reader closure and schema_version_reader for branchless-activation guard. Generic trait impl TaskOperationInteractor<S>: TaskOperationService normalizes to bare type name after T043 type-param stripping.

informal_grounds.summary: T040 CLI thinning — TaskOperationInteractor is a pre-existing generic interactor modified to accept injected branch_reader and schema_version_reader closures instead of direct infrastructure imports, completing the CLI-to-usecase dependency isolation for task mutation operations

T040 CLI thinning — error type for TypeSignalsService::run; aggregates port-level failures (invalid track-id, status read failure, frozen-track guard, layer-bindings load failure, per-layer evaluation failure) surfaced to the CLI without leaking infrastructure details

TypeSignalsInteractor.docs: Interactor implementing TypeSignalsService. Orchestrates track-status guard, layer-bindings resolution, and per-layer signal evaluation. All I/O is performed via injected ports (no direct infrastructure calls).

informal_grounds.summary: T040 CLI thinning — usecase application-service interactor for `sotp track type-signals`; moves track-status guard, layer-bindings resolution, and per-layer signal evaluation orchestration out of the CLI handler into the usecase layer so the CLI becomes a thin adapter
T040 CLI thinning — CQRS Command carrying all input parameters for TypeSignalsService::run including lenient flag; enables the CLI handler to forward flags without embedding orchestration logic

CatalogueImplSignalsInteractor.docs: Interactor implementing CatalogueImplSignalsService. Orchestrates per-layer A/B/C TypeGraph fetch, signal evaluator invocation, and region-by-region result formatting. Injected dependencies: CatalogueDocumentLoaderPort (A-side catalogue file load), CatalogueToExtendedCratePort (A-side CatalogueDocument → ExtendedCrate conversion), SignalEvaluatorPort (Phase 1 + Phase 2 evaluation), RustdocCratePort (B-side baseline load via load_from_path; C-side live capture via capture_current), TdddLayerBindingsPort (architecture-rules.json enumeration), SymlinkGuardPort (symlink stat checks — keeps usecase free of direct std::fs I/O per hexagonal-purity rule). apps/cli constructs the concrete infrastructure adapters (FsCatalogueDocumentLoader, CatalogueToExtendedCrateCodec, SignalEvaluatorV2, RustdocCrateAdapter, FsTdddLayerBindingsAdapter, FsSymlinkGuard) and injects them at the composition root. The 6th port (SymlinkGuardPort) is the proper-layering realization that IN-18 mandates (hexagonal purity: no raw std::fs I/O in the usecase layer); surfaced by a Codex PR-review finding. [source: ADR 2026-05-11-2330 D2]

CatalogueImplSignalsError.docs: Error type for CatalogueImplSignalsService::run. Variants: InvalidTrackId (track id format invalid), LayerBindingsLoad (loading TDDD layer bindings from architecture-rules.json failed; covers both file-read/parse failure and requested layer not found or not tddd.enabled), CatalogueLoad (A-side catalogue document read/decode failure), BaselineLoad (B-side baseline rustdoc load failure), ExtendedCrateConversion (A-side CatalogueDocument → ExtendedCrate conversion failure), SchemaExport (C-side live capture failure), Evaluation (signal evaluator failure), SymlinkRejected (path traversal or symlink guard rejection from the security validation in the interactor), NoLayers (no TDDD-enabled layers found or resolved). [source: ADR 2026-05-11-2330 D2]

RenderContractMapCommand.docs: Command input for RenderContractMap::execute. Modified: removed stale kind_filter field (baseline was captured before T003 removed it); current shape is 2 fields (track_id, layer_filter).

RenderContractMapError.docs: Error variants for RenderContractMap::execute. Modified: removed stale UnknownKindFilter variant (baseline was captured before T003 removed it); corrected variant payloads to match actual enum (tuple and struct variants, not unit). thiserror #[from] generates From<CatalogueLoaderError> and From<ContractMapWriterError>; declared with explicit generic_args to disambiguate the two impls. Also Display/Error for thiserror-generated impls.

BaselineCaptureService.docs: Application service (driving port) for the bin/sotp track baseline-capture use case. Orchestrates symlink guards, track-id validation, layer-bindings resolution, and per-layer rustdoc baseline capture.

informal_grounds.summary: T040 CLI thinning — ApplicationService driving port for the baseline-capture use case; makes orchestration logic testable via the interactor pattern and separates it from the CLI handler

CatalogueImplSignalsService.docs: Application service (driving port) for the bin/sotp track catalogue-impl-signals use case. Returns a CatalogueImplSignalsReport (text + any_red flag). Modified in T040 addendum: return type changed from Result<String, ...> to Result<CatalogueImplSignalsReport, ...>. [source: ADR 2026-05-11-2330 D2]

TypeSignalsService.docs: Application service trait for the sotp track type-signals use case. Orchestrates track-ID validation, track-status guard (active-track check), layer-bindings resolution, and per-layer signal evaluation.

informal_grounds.summary: T040 CLI thinning — ApplicationService driving port for the type-signals use case; enables the TypeSignalsInteractor to be injected and tested independently of the CLI handler; active-track guard is enforced inside the interactor via TrackStatusReaderPort

TrackBlobReader.docs: Secondary port for reading track-level domain documents from an external source (git ref, filesystem). All 8 trait methods are declared here for full structural equality. Modified in T021 (read_type_signals added), T022 (read_type_catalogue return type changed to BlobFetchResult<(Vec<u8>, String)>), T024 (read_catalogue_for_spec_ref_check changed to BlobFetchResult<(CatalogueDocument, String)>). Methods with default implementations (read_enabled_layers, read_catalogue_for_spec_ref_check, read_catalogue_spec_signals_document, read_catalogue_spec_signal_opted_in_layers, read_type_signals) are declared with has_default_impl: true and underscore-prefixed param names matching the trait default impl signatures (T033 done, has_body asymmetry resolved for this entry). BlobFetchResult type parameters use last-segment names per L1 short-name reduction: SpecDocument, ImplPlanDocument, TypeSignalsDocument, CatalogueSpecSignalsDocument are all resolvable in usecase scope. CatalogueDocument is cross-crate and declared with its qualified path. IN-27: resolves the method count/name mismatch (partial-declaration issue) for this trait — all 8 methods are now declared. This entry is 🔵-equivalent pending T031 finalization (which re-verifies post-T033 changes). The remaining AC-13 blocker is check_strict_merge_gate (where_predicates gap, see Gap 3 / T035).

CatalogueSpecSignalsWriter.docs: Existing secondary_port for persisting catalogue-spec signal documents. Referenced to expose implements edge for FsCatalogueSpecSignalsStore.

PreCommitTypeSignalsService.docs: Existing application_service trait for running pre-commit type signal checks. Referenced for continuity.

ReviewCheckApprovedService.docs: Pre-existing application service trait for review check-approved. Referenced to expose ReviewCheckApprovedInteractor implements edge.

TaskOperationService.docs: Pre-existing application service trait for task mutation operations. Referenced to expose TaskOperationInteractor implements edge.

SchemaExporterPort.docs: Existing secondary_port for exporting crate schema as JSON string. Referenced to expose implements edge for RustdocSchemaExporter.

SpecElementHashReader.docs: Secondary port for reading per-element SHA-256 digests from spec.json on a remote git ref (usecase::catalogue_spec_refs). Referenced to expose the GitShowTrackBlobReader -.impl.-> SpecElementHashReader edge in the contract map. Pre-existing baseline port, not modified in this track. BlobFetchResult is parameterised with the concrete BTreeMap return type (R8 full-generic declaration).

usecase::merge_gate::check_strict_merge_.docs: IN-23/IN-20/IN-22: Strict merge gate orchestration. Actual Rust signature: pub fn check_strict_merge_gate<R>(branch: &str, reader: &R) -> VerifyOutcome where R: TrackBlobReader + SpecElementHashReader. Modified in T022 (Stage 2 rewired to read_type_signals returning TypeSignalsDocument) and T024 (Stage 3 migrated to read_catalogue_for_spec_ref_check returning BlobFetchResult<(CatalogueDocument, String)>). The return type is domain::verify::VerifyOutcome (FQN path; L1 reduces to 'VerifyOutcome' matching C-side rustdoc). Generics structural equality requires T035 (where-form normalization) to land first: C-side rustdoc emits `where R: TrackBlobReader + SpecElementHashReader` in where-predicate form, while A-codec emits inline bounds before T035. After T035 normalizes A-codec output to where form, post-T035+T031 re-finalization will achieve structural equality for this entry.
